seoTitle: How to Insert a Table in Slack (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Build a data table inside Slack using Canvas and the /table command, or use Lists for a tracking table with due dates and assignees.
intro: Sharing structured data in Slack shouldn't turn into unreadable plain text. This guide covers building a quick table inside a Canvas using the /table command, plus using Slack's Lists tool for more powerful project tracking tables.

steps.heading: Open a Canvas in your channel
steps.body: Click the Canvas header icon inside a target channel to open a collaborative document space.

steps.heading: Generate a table with a slash command
steps.body: Click into the blank canvas and type /table to trigger a customizable multi-column grid.

steps.heading: Fill in your data
steps.body: Type text into cells and use the Tab key to move quickly across columns.

steps.heading: Add rows and columns as needed
steps.body: Hover near the grid borders and click the edge menu bars to insert new rows or columns.

steps.heading: Use Lists for advanced tracking
steps.body: Open the sidebar Files section, select List, and build a dynamic table with due dates and assignees for ongoing project tracking.

faq.question: What's the difference between a Canvas table and a List?
faq.answer: A Canvas table is a simple static grid for sharing data, while Lists offer filterable, structured tracking with fields like assignees and due dates.

faq.question: Can I pin a table to the top of a channel?
faq.answer: Yes, you can pin the finalized canvas containing your table directly to the channel header.

faq.question: Can I export a table to a spreadsheet?
faq.answer: Some canvas tables support exporting to CSV for use in external spreadsheet tools.

faq.question: Do all channel members see the same table in real time?
faq.answer: Yes, both Canvas tables and Lists update in real time for everyone with access to the channel.
